streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: phenylacetylene as dipolarophile in separate stream for controlled stoichiometry. Flow rate set to deliver limiting reagent at 0.0333 mmol/min.
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: ethyl nitroacetate at 2x concentration to achieve 2.0 equiv stoichiometry. Higher flow rate compensates for 2x stoichiometry requirement.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Both streams mixed rapidly at T-mixer before heated coil. DES preheated to 60-80°C to reduce viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT). Rapid mixing critical to prevent nitrile oxide dimerization.
pre_reactor_steps: Preheat both feed lines to 70°C to reduce DES viscosity for reliable pumping and mixing
post_reactor_steps: Cool product stream to room temperature
Collect for offline workup (water quench, DCM extraction per batch protocol)
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with in-situ nitrile oxide generation. Rate-limiting step is thermal dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C. DES acts as both solvent and promoter. Critical design factors: (1) Rapid mixing to prevent nitrile oxide dimerization, (2) Precise temperature control at 120°C, (3) DES viscosity management via preheating, (4) BPR required to maintain DES stability and prevent volatile loss. Calculation shows Pe=17 indicating significant axial dispersion - reactor behaves closer to CSTR than PFR but still achieves target conversion.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 1.5 min (range 1.0-2.2 min) based on 10x intensification factor from batch 15 min reaction time. No analogies available.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: total flow rate 0.5 mL/min from reactor volume 0.75 mL / residence time 1.5 min. Stream A: 0.167 mL/min, Stream B: 0.333 mL/min.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch 120°C - optimal for nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate in DES. Chemistry Plan confirms this temperature is critical.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation: reactor concentration 0.067 M after mixing (dilution from two streams). Maintains reasonable concentration for reaction kinetics.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar - gas-liquid system with potential volatile loss of DES components at 120°C. BPR maintains system stability.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Chemistry Plan specifies coil reactor for thermal reaction. Provides good heat transfer at 120°C.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature stability (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES. Not photochemical so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation used 1.0 mm ID giving Re=11 (laminar flow) and reasonable pressure drop. Balances mixing and heat transfer.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: 0.75 mL from engineering design to achieve target residence time with available flow rates.
